rationale: Major frictions: the archetype's muted dark/earth palette (navy, ochre, green) and detailed cross-section animation style clash hard with Crowd City's bright primary-color, low-poly stickman aesthetic, and "hidden bunkers" curiosity bait has no plausible bridge to a swarm/consume crowd loop. The only weak overlap is the Eiffel Tower landmark (which Crowd City does feature) and a broad casual curiosity-driven audience — but you'd need to fully reskin the hook into "what's hiding beneath the city your crowd is taking over?" to make it work, which is a heavy creative lift on a single-advertiser, low-velocity reference. Recommend skipping unless testing a bespoke landmark-reveal variant tied directly to crowd gameplay.
